title: Compiler Principles Lab (编译原理实验)
summary: A C++ course project implementing a lexical analyzer and an LL(1) parser. The hollowed task now exposes fine-grained progressive units for the mainline course logic inside LexicalAnalysis.cpp and SyntacticParser.cpp only.
instructionPreview: This project implements two classic compiler front-end components in C++: a lexical analyzer and an LL(1) syntactic parser. The code compiles with g++ and runs as standalone command-line programs.
instruction:
This project implements two classic compiler front-end components in C++: a lexical analyzer and an LL(1) syntactic parser. The code compiles with g++ and runs as standalone command-line programs.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is already present under `/workspace/LexicalAnalyzer/` and `/workspace/Parser/`. You need to implement all the progressive mainline modules listed below so that all tests pass.

## Progressive Units

### Lexical Analyzer (`LexicalAnalyzer/LexicalAnalysis.cpp`)
- `lex_preprocess_io`: file-reading/output skeleton for `preProcess`
- `lex_comment_stripping`: remove `//` and `/* ... */` comments
- `lex_whitespace_normalization`: remove newlines/tabs and collapse repeated spaces
- `lex_preprocessing`: final preprocessor reporting and cleaned-file output
- `lex_identifier_rules`: validate identifiers
- `lex_number_rules`: validate decimal integers
- `lex_classification`: map reserved words/punctuation and helper-based token classes
- `lex_error_locator`: locate illegal tokens in the original source
- `lex_token_scan_loop`: scan the normalized stream into `wtPair` records
- `lex_token_table_print`: print the legal-token table to stdout
- `lex_token_error_report`: deduplicate/report illegal tokens with source locations
- `lex_tokenizer`: write the token table and illegal-token report to `processed.txt`

### LL(1) Syntactic Parser (`Parser/SyntacticParser.cpp`)
- `parser_load_file`: read the grammar file and echo its contents
- `parser_rule_parser`: extract rules, non-terminals, terminals, and right parts
- `parser_rule_formatter`: stream a production as `A::=...|...`
- `parser_grammar_io`: print extracted rules and symbol sets
- `parser_first_formatter`: stream FIRST/FOLLOW sets in the lab format
- `parser_first_core`: recursively compute FIRST sets
- `parser_first_sets`: print FIRST sets for each right-hand side
- `parser_follow_core`: compute FOLLOW sets with recursive propagation
- `parser_follow_sets`: print FOLLOW sets for the non-terminal set
- `parser_ll1_header`: initialize LL(1) rows, columns, and empty cells
- `parser_ll1_builder`: populate LL(1) cells from FIRST/FOLLOW results
- `parser_ll1_table`: print the completed LL(1) table
- `parser_parse_state`: print the analysis stack/input stack trace rows
- `parser_analysis_init`: bootstrap the parse stacks and trace header
- `parser_analysis`: run the LL(1) parse loop and final acceptance/rejection output

## Test-Coupled Signatures

The following functions are called directly by the test suite (via compiled binary execution). You must implement them with these exact signatures:

- `bool preProcess(const string fileName, const string newFileName)` — Preprocesses the source file by removing comments, normalizing whitespace, and writing the cleaned output
- `bool mainProcess(const string fileName, const string fileNameProcessed)` — Tokenizes preprocessed code, reports illegal identifiers, and writes the token table output file
- `bool IsIdentifier(const string word)` — Determines whether a word is a valid identifier
- `bool IsNum(const string word)` — Determines whether a word is a valid decimal integer
- `int wtMatch(const string word)` — Classifies a word into its token category code
- `string findError(const string fileName, const string value)` — Locates token occurrences in the original source and returns row/column information
- `string load_file(string file_name)` — Reads the grammar file and returns its contents as a string
- `void split_into_rules(const string text, vector<rule>& rules, set<char>& non_terminal, set<char>& terminal, set<string>& right_parts)` — Parses grammar text into rules and symbol sets
- `ostream& operator<<(ostream& out, rule rule)` — Formats a grammar rule for display
- `void check_rules(const vector<rule> rules)` — Prints the extracted production rules
- `template <class T> void check_set(set<T> set_, string tip)` — Prints extracted symbol sets with a heading
- `ostream& operator<<(ostream& out, F_set f_set)` — Formats a FIRST or FOLLOW set for output
- `F_set findFirst(const string ss, vector<rule> rules)` — Computes the FIRST set for a right-hand-side string
- `vector<F_set> checkFirst(set<string> right_parts, vector<rule> rules)` — Computes and prints FIRST sets for all grammar right-hand sides
- `F_set findFollow(const char non_terminal, vector<rule> rules)` — Computes the FOLLOW set for a non-terminal
- `vector<F_set> checkFollow(set<char> non_terminal, vector<rule> rules)` — Computes and prints FOLLOW sets for all non-terminals
- `LL_1_table LL_1(set<char>& terminal, set<char>& non_terminal, vector<rule> rules)` — Constructs the LL(1) parsing table
- `ostream& operator<<(ostream& out, LL_1_table table)` — Formats the LL(1) parsing table for display
- `void print_state(stack<char> analysis_s, stack<char> input_s, rule& rule_temp)` — Prints the current parse trace row
- `void syntacticAnalysis(LL_1_table table)` — Performs LL(1) table-driven parsing on user input

moduleDag.project: Compiler Principles Lab (编译原理实验)
moduleDag.description: A C++ course project implementing a lexical analyzer and an LL(1) parser. The hollowed task now exposes fine-grained progressive units for the mainline course logic inside LexicalAnalysis.cpp and SyntacticParser.cpp only.

nodes.label: Preprocessor File Loader
nodes.description: Build the initial `preProcess` I/O skeleton that reads the source file into memory and writes an intermediate file so later cleanup stages can extend the same function.

nodes.label: Comment Stripper
nodes.description: Extend `preProcess` so it removes both single-line `//` comments and block `/* ... */` comments before any whitespace normalization happens.

nodes.label: Whitespace Normalizer
nodes.description: Add newline stripping, tab stripping, and repeated-space collapsing inside `preProcess` once comment removal is in place.

nodes.label: Source Code Preprocessor
nodes.description: Finalize `preProcess` with the lab-style progress output and write the cleaned single-line code to `afterPreProcess.txt`.

nodes.label: Identifier Validator
nodes.description: Implement `IsIdentifier` so identifiers must start with a letter or underscore and continue with alphanumerics or underscores only.

nodes.label: Integer Validator
nodes.description: Implement `IsNum` as the decimal-integer checker used by token classification.

nodes.label: Token Classifier
nodes.description: Implement `wtMatch` by mapping reserved words/punctuation to course token codes and delegating identifier/number checks to the helper validators.

nodes.label: Illegal Token Locator
nodes.description: Implement `findError` so illegal token spellings can be located by original row/column positions in the source file.

nodes.label: Tokenizer Scan Loop
nodes.description: Implement the core scanning loop in `mainProcess`: read the preprocessed buffer, split words versus punctuation, and accumulate token records.

nodes.label: Token Table Printer
nodes.description: Add the stdout token-table rendering stage in `mainProcess` and collect the illegal spellings that are filtered out of the regular token table.

nodes.label: Illegal Token Reporter
nodes.description: Deduplicate illegal token spellings and print their token-order positions plus original-source locations via `findError`.

nodes.label: Tokenizer and Error Reporter
nodes.description: Finish `mainProcess` by writing the token table plus illegal-token report to `processed.txt` and printing the completion message.

nodes.label: Grammar File Loader
nodes.description: Implement `load_file` so the parser can read a grammar file into memory and print the file contents before parsing it.

nodes.label: Grammar Rule Parser
nodes.description: Implement `split_into_rules` so the raw grammar text is decomposed into rules, non-terminals, terminals, and right-hand-side strings.

nodes.label: Grammar Rule Formatter
nodes.description: Implement `operator<<(rule)` so grammar rules and table cells render as `A::=...|...` strings.

nodes.label: Grammar Reporter
nodes.description: Add `check_rules` and `check_set` diagnostics so the extracted grammar structures are printed before set computation begins.

nodes.label: FIRST/FOLLOW Formatter
nodes.description: Implement `operator<<(F_set)` so FIRST and FOLLOW sets share a common printable representation.

nodes.label: FIRST Set Core
nodes.description: Implement recursive FIRST-set computation with terminal detection, non-terminal expansion, and epsilon propagation.

nodes.label: FIRST Set Reporter
nodes.description: Implement `checkFirst` so every right-hand side gets its printed FIRST-set line in the parser diagnostics.

nodes.label: FOLLOW Set Core
nodes.description: Implement FOLLOW-set propagation across rule tails, recursive parent inheritance, and end-marker insertion.

nodes.label: FOLLOW Set Reporter
nodes.description: Implement `checkFollow` so the parser prints FOLLOW sets for the non-terminal set.

nodes.label: LL(1) Table Initializer
nodes.description: Initialize the LL(1) table headers and empty cells from the terminal/non-terminal sets before rule population begins.

nodes.label: LL(1) Table Builder
nodes.description: Populate the LL(1) table by expanding FIRST sets and, when needed, FOLLOW sets for epsilon productions.

nodes.label: LL(1) Table Formatter
nodes.description: Implement `operator<<(LL_1_table)` so the finished parse table is rendered as a grid for the lab output.

nodes.label: Parse State Printer
nodes.description: Implement `print_state` so each parse step prints the analysis stack, remaining input, and chosen production.

nodes.label: Parser Stack Bootstrap
nodes.description: Bootstrap the parser input string, initialize the analysis/input stacks, and print the parse trace header.

nodes.label: LL(1) Table-Driven Parser
nodes.description: Finish `syntacticAnalysis` with the LL(1) parse loop, non-terminal expansion, terminal matching, and final acceptance/rejection output.

edges.label: comment removal extends the base preProcess file I/O skeleton
whitespace cleanup runs on the comment-free buffer produced inside preProcess
the final preprocessing stage prints and writes the fully normalized buffer
mainProcess tokenizes the single-line file emitted by preProcess
wtMatch delegates identifier recognition to IsIdentifier
wtMatch delegates integer recognition to IsNum
the token scan loop classifies every extracted lexeme through wtMatch
table printing consumes the wtSeq token sequence built by the scan loop
illegal-token reporting reuses the errorSeq collected while printing the token table
illegal-token reporting resolves row/column locations via findError
the final tokenizer stage writes the same table/report content to processed.txt
split_into_rules parses the grammar text loaded from disk
grammar diagnostics print the extracted rules and symbol sets created by split_into_rules
check_rules streams each production through operator<<(rule)
findFirst expands the vector<rule> produced by split_into_rules
checkFirst prints each FIRST result through operator<<(F_set)
checkFirst reports the recursive FIRST sets computed by findFirst
findFollow scans rule bodies produced by split_into_rules
findFollow uses FIRST sets of trailing symbols via findFirst
checkFollow prints F_set values through the shared formatter
checkFollow reports the FOLLOW sets computed by findFollow
LL(1) population starts from the initialized row/column grid
LL(1) cells are selected from FIRST sets of production right-hand sides
epsilon productions use FOLLOW sets to fill additional LL(1) cells
the LL(1) builder iterates across the parsed grammar rules
LL(1) table cells stream rule values through operator<<(rule)
the table formatter renders the completed LL(1) structure
print_state streams the active production through operator<<(rule)
the parse loop prints every state transition via print_state
the parse loop consumes the initialized stacks and status flag created at parser bootstrap
syntacticAnalysis consults the completed LL(1) table for every non-terminal expansion
